walkthrough.title: Place the Game Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get Eldritch Horror set up. First, unfold the large game board and place it in the center of the table. This is a map of the world with named cities connected by colored paths. You will see train paths in brown and ship paths in blue. There are also numbered wilderness and sea spaces between the cities. Make sure everyone can reach the board comfortably because you will all be placing tokens on it throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Create the Gate Stack
walkthrough.text: Find the nine Gate tokens. These are round tokens that represent tears in the fabric of reality. Randomize them and stack them facedown, common side up, within reach of all players. Gates are how doom advances during the game, so you will be interacting with this stack frequently.

walkthrough.title: Create the Clue Pool
walkthrough.text: Take all 36 Clue tokens and place them facedown in a pile, common side up. Randomize them so nobody knows which space each Clue points to. Clues are one of your most important resources. You will use them to reroll dice during tests, solve Mysteries, and solve Rumors. Keep this pool within reach of all players.

walkthrough.title: Organize the General Token Pool
walkthrough.text: Now gather all the remaining tokens and sort them into piles near the board. You need Health tokens in two sizes: 30 tokens worth 1 Health and 12 tokens worth 3 Health. Sanity tokens come in the same sizes: 24 worth 1 Sanity and 12 worth 3 Sanity. Also set out the 30 Improvement tokens, 20 Travel Tickets (8 train and 12 ship), 20 Eldritch tokens, the Mystery token, and the 4 Rumor tokens. Place them all where everyone can reach.

walkthrough.title: Choose the Ancient One
walkthrough.text: As a group, choose one of the four Ancient One sheets. Each one provides a completely different game experience with unique Mysteries, Cultist monsters, and special rules. For your first game, it is recommended you choose Azathoth, the Daemon Sultan, because it is the most straightforward Ancient One. Place the chosen sheet faceup near the game board, displaying the Doom value in the top-left corner, and resolve any setup effects printed on the sheet.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Doom and Omen Tracks
walkthrough.text: Place the Doom token on the space of the Doom track matching the number in the top-left corner of your Ancient One sheet. For Azathoth, this is typically space 15. Place the Omen token on the green space of the Omen track. If the Ancient One has a setup effect that places Eldritch tokens on the Omen track, do that now. The Doom track counts down toward zero. When it hits zero, the Ancient One awakens and things get very bad.

walkthrough.title: Create the Monster Cup
walkthrough.text: Take all 34 normal (non-epic) Monster tokens and place them in an opaque container. A bowl, mug, or the game box lid works great. Shake it to randomize the tokens. Do not put the 9 Epic Monster tokens in the cup. Epic Monsters are identified by their red color and clipped lower-left corner. Set those aside because they are only spawned by specific game effects that call them out by name.

walkthrough.title: Separate Ancient One Cards
walkthrough.text: This is important. Take all Research Encounter cards, Special Encounter cards, and Mystery cards that do not correspond to your chosen Ancient One and return them to the game box. Each Ancient One has its own Mystery deck and Research Encounter deck, identifiable by the Ancient One illustration on the card back. Some Ancient Ones also have a Special Encounter deck noted in their setup effect. Only cards matching your Ancient One stay in the game.

walkthrough.title: Separate and Place All Other Decks
walkthrough.text: Now sort the remaining cards by their backs and shuffle each deck separately. Shuffle all Expedition Encounter cards into one deck regardless of card back. Shuffle the Spell cards and Condition cards into two separate decks and place them faceup with the top card visible. Separate the location encounter cards by region: America, Europe, Asia/Australia, and General. Place the Asset deck and Artifact deck nearby. Set the Mythos cards aside for the next step.

walkthrough.title: Build the Mythos Deck
walkthrough.text: Follow the instructions at the bottom of your Ancient One sheet to build the Mythos deck. The sheet lists how many cards of each color and stage to include. Mythos cards come in three colors: green, yellow, and blue. You will build the deck in three stages, shuffling each stage separately, then stacking Stage III on the bottom, Stage II in the middle, and Stage I on top. This ensures the game ramps up in difficulty. Return unused Mythos cards to the box.

walkthrough.title: Choose the Lead Investigator
walkthrough.text: Players agree on who receives the Lead Investigator token. If you cannot decide, assign it randomly, such as by rolling a die. The Lead Investigator always goes first in each phase and makes final decisions on any disputes. This token can pass to other players during the game if the Lead Investigator is defeated.

walkthrough.title: Choose and Place Investigators
walkthrough.text: Starting with the Lead Investigator and going clockwise, each player chooses one of the 12 investigators. Take the matching Investigator sheet and place the matching Investigator token on the game board space listed on the back of the sheet. Each investigator has unique stats in five skills: Lore, Influence, Observation, Strength, and Will, plus a special ability.

walkthrough.title: Collect Starting Possessions
walkthrough.text: Each investigator receives the starting possessions listed on the back of their Investigator sheet. This may include Asset cards, Artifact cards, Spells, Conditions, Clues, or travel tickets. Take the matching cards from the game box or relevant decks and place them near your Investigator sheet. Then take Health and Sanity tokens equal to your investigator's maximum Health and Sanity as listed on the front of the sheet.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Reserve
walkthrough.text: Place the top four cards of the Asset deck faceup in the four slots of the reserve area at the bottom-left of the game board. These are the Assets available for purchase during the game through the Acquire Assets action.

walkthrough.title: Spawn Starting Gates, Clues, and Monsters
walkthrough.text: Check the Reference card for your player count. It tells you how many starting Gates and Clues to spawn. Spawn Gates by drawing Gate tokens from the stack and placing them faceup on the indicated spaces. Each time a Gate spawns, also draw one random Monster from the Monster cup and place it on the same space. Then spawn the listed number of Clues by drawing Clue tokens and placing them facedown on the spaces indicated on their backs.

walkthrough.title: Draw the First Mystery
walkthrough.text: Draw one Mystery card from your Ancient One's Mystery deck and place it faceup next to the Ancient One sheet. This is your first objective. Read it aloud so everyone knows what you need to accomplish. You need to solve three Mysteries total to win the game. When one is solved, you immediately draw the next. Place the Active Expedition token on the space matching the top card of the Expedition Encounter deck. You are now ready to play.

These are available for the Acquire Assets action

Read it aloud — this is your first objective
Place Active Expedition token on matching space
You need to solve 3 Mysteries to win

walkthrough.title: Game Round Overview
walkthrough.text: Eldritch Horror is played over a series of rounds. Each round has three phases that always happen in the same order: the Action Phase, the Encounter Phase, and the Mythos Phase. All investigators resolve each phase together before moving on to the next. The game continues round after round until the investigators either win by solving three Mysteries or lose when the Ancient One overwhelms them.

walkthrough.title: Phase 1: The Action Phase
walkthrough.text: During the Action Phase, each investigator performs two actions, starting with the Lead Investigator and proceeding clockwise. There are six possible actions: Travel, Rest, Trade, Prepare for Travel, Acquire Assets, and Component Actions. Each investigator can only perform each action once per round. For example, you cannot Travel twice, but you can Travel and then Rest.

walkthrough.title: Action: Travel
walkthrough.text: The Travel action lets you move your investigator token to any adjacent space on the board. Spaces are connected by paths, which come in two types: brown train paths and blue ship paths. After your initial move, you may spend travel tickets to move additional spaces. Spend a Train Ticket to move along a train path, or a Ship Ticket to move along a ship path. You can spend multiple tickets in one Travel action to cover more ground.

walkthrough.title: Action: Rest
walkthrough.text: The Rest action lets you recover 1 Health and 1 Sanity. Take one of each from the token pool and add them to your investigator. You cannot exceed your maximum Health or Sanity. Important: you cannot perform the Rest action if there is a Monster on your space. If a Monster is blocking you, you will have to deal with it during the Encounter Phase before you can rest next round.

walkthrough.title: Action: Trade
walkthrough.text: The Trade action lets you exchange possessions with another investigator on the same space. You can trade Assets, Artifacts, Clues, Spells, and travel tickets. Both players must agree to the trade. This is useful for getting key items to the investigator who needs them most, like passing a weapon to your fighter or a Spell to your scholar.

walkthrough.title: Action: Prepare for Travel
walkthrough.text: If your investigator is on a City space, you may take the Prepare for Travel action to gain one travel ticket of your choice. You can only gain a Train Ticket if there is at least one train path connected to your space, and a Ship Ticket if there is at least one ship path connected to your space. You cannot hold more than two travel tickets at a time. If you ever exceed two, you must discard down to two.

walkthrough.title: Action: Acquire Assets
walkthrough.text: If your investigator is on a City space with no Monster present, you may attempt to acquire Asset cards from the reserve. Test your Influence skill by rolling dice. Each die that shows a 5 or 6 is a success. You may then take Asset cards from the four faceup reserve slots whose total value is equal to or less than your number of successes. If you gain no cards, you may discard one card from the reserve. After the action, refill any empty reserve slots from the Asset deck. You may also take a Bank Loan to gain 2 extra successes, but you receive a Debt Condition.

walkthrough.title: Action: Component Actions
walkthrough.text: Component Actions are special actions printed on your Investigator sheet, Asset cards, Artifact cards, Spells, or Conditions. They are always preceded by the word Action in bold. You may perform one Component Action from one of your components. You cannot use the same component's action twice per round, but you can use actions from different components. Some components also have Local Actions, which another investigator on the same space can use.

walkthrough.title: Phase 2: The Encounter Phase
walkthrough.text: After all investigators have taken their actions, the Encounter Phase begins. Starting with the Lead Investigator and going clockwise, each investigator resolves one encounter. If there is a Monster on your space, you must resolve a Combat Encounter against each Monster before doing anything else. If there are no Monsters, you choose either a location encounter or a token encounter.

walkthrough.title: Location Encounters
walkthrough.text: For a location encounter, draw a card from the encounter deck matching your space's region: America, Europe, Asia/Australia, or General. Find the effect matching your specific space or type of space, read the narrative, and resolve the tests and effects described. City spaces typically let you draw from the General Encounter deck as well. The text below each named city on the board hints at the most common effect found in its encounters.

walkthrough.title: Token Encounters
walkthrough.text: Instead of a location encounter, you may encounter a token on your space. Clue tokens let you draw a Research Encounter card specific to your Ancient One. Gate tokens let you draw an Other World Encounter card. The Active Expedition token lets you draw an Expedition Encounter card. Rumor tokens let you resolve the encounter listed on the corresponding Rumor Mythos card. These token encounters are often more rewarding but also more dangerous.

walkthrough.title: Combat Encounters
walkthrough.text: When you encounter a Monster, flip the Monster token facedown to read its information. Combat has two tests resolved in order. First, resolve a Will test. If the Monster's horror value exceeds your successes, you lose Sanity equal to the difference. Second, resolve a Strength test. If the Monster's damage value exceeds your successes, you lose Health equal to the difference. The Monster loses Health equal to your Strength test successes. When a Monster has lost Health equal to or greater than its toughness, it is defeated and returned to the Monster cup.

walkthrough.title: How Tests Work
walkthrough.text: Many effects require you to resolve a test. Roll a number of dice equal to your skill value in the tested skill, plus or minus any modifiers. Each die showing a 5 or 6 is a success. You always roll at least 1 die. After rolling, you may spend Clue tokens to reroll individual dice, one Clue per reroll. You can reroll as many times as you have Clues to spend. Only one card effect that provides a skill bonus may be used per test.

walkthrough.title: Complex Encounters
walkthrough.text: Expedition Encounters, Other World Encounters, and Special Encounters are complex encounters. Each card has three boxes: an initial effect at the top, a pass effect, and a fail effect. Resolve the initial effect first, usually a test. If you pass, resolve the pass effect. If you fail, resolve the fail effect. These encounters can be very rewarding, such as closing a Gate or gaining powerful items, but failing can be devastating. Other World Encounters are how you close Gates: if the card instructs you to close this Gate, discard the Gate token from your space.

walkthrough.title: Phase 3: The Mythos Phase
walkthrough.text: After all encounters are resolved, the Lead Investigator draws and resolves the top card of the Mythos deck. Mythos cards have up to seven possible effects resolved in order from left to right, top to bottom. Not every Mythos card has all seven effects. You only resolve the icons shown on the current card. The possible effects are: Advance Omen, Resolve Reckoning, Spawn Gates, Monster Surge, Spawn Clues, Place Rumor Token, Place Eldritch Tokens, and the card's text effect.

walkthrough.title: Advance Omen and Doom
walkthrough.text: When a Mythos card shows the Advance Omen icon, move the Omen token one space clockwise around the Omen track. Then advance Doom by 1 for each Gate currently on the game board that matches the Omen token's new symbol. This is the primary way Doom advances. The more Gates on the board, the faster Doom falls. Closing Gates is critical to slowing the Ancient One's approach.

walkthrough.title: Reckoning, Gates, and Monster Surge
walkthrough.text: Reckoning resolves all effects marked with a red reckoning icon on components in play: Monsters first, then the Ancient One sheet, then ongoing Mythos cards, then investigator possessions and Conditions. Spawn Gates draws Gate tokens from the stack and places them on the indicated spaces; each new Gate also spawns one Monster on the same space. Monster Surge spawns additional Monsters at Gates matching the current Omen symbol.

walkthrough.title: Mythos Card Text Effects
walkthrough.text: The bottom of each Mythos card has a text effect. If the card says Event, resolve the effect immediately and discard the card. If it says Ongoing, place the card near the Ancient One sheet. It stays in play affecting the game until it is discarded by a game effect or solved. Ongoing Mythos cards often include Rumor effects that place Rumor tokens on the board and create side quests the investigators need to resolve before something terrible happens.

walkthrough.title: Mysteries and Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: To win, investigators must solve three of the Ancient One's Mysteries. Each Mystery card describes a unique condition for solving it, often involving spending Clues, placing Eldritch tokens, closing Gates at the right time, or defeating specific Monsters. As soon as a Mystery is solved, draw a new one from the deck. Once three are solved, the investigators immediately win. Read each Mystery carefully and coordinate your team's efforts toward completing them.

walkthrough.title: The Ancient One Awakens
walkthrough.text: If the Doom token reaches the 0 space of the Doom track, the Ancient One awakens. Flip the Ancient One sheet to its back and resolve any Awakens effects. The back of the sheet describes new rules, including how the investigators can now lose the game and a Final Mystery they must solve in addition to three regular Mysteries. After the Ancient One awakens, defeated investigators are permanently eliminated instead of being replaced. For Azathoth specifically, when he awakens the world is devoured and the investigators immediately lose.

walkthrough.title: Losing the Game
walkthrough.text: Investigators lose if any of the following occurs: the Doom token reaches 0 and the Ancient One's awakening effect causes an immediate loss (as with Azathoth), all investigators are eliminated from the game after the Ancient One awakens, a card effect explicitly causes the investigators to lose (usually a Rumor Mythos card), or the Mythos deck runs out and you are required to draw a card. Stay vigilant about Doom, keep investigators alive, and do not ignore Rumors.

walkthrough.title: Health, Sanity, and Defeated Investigators
walkthrough.text: When an investigator reaches zero Health or zero Sanity, they are immediately defeated. Doom advances by 1. The investigator token is moved to the nearest City space and laid on its side with a Health or Sanity token to indicate the cause. All Conditions, Health, Sanity, and Improvement tokens are discarded, but possessions are placed on the sheet for another investigator to retrieve. At the end of the Mythos Phase, the defeated player chooses a new investigator from the unused pile.

walkthrough.title: Improvement Tokens and Skills
walkthrough.text: Investigators can improve their skills through certain encounter effects. When you improve a skill, take an Improvement token with the matching icon and place it below your Investigator sheet with the plus-1 side up. If you improve that skill a second time, flip the token to the plus-2 side. Your skill value equals the number printed on your sheet plus your Improvement bonus. Certain named cities on the board are associated with specific skill improvements: San Francisco for Observation, Istanbul for Influence, Rome for Will, Shanghai for Lore, and Sydney for Strength.

walkthrough.title: Spells and Conditions
walkthrough.text: Spells and Conditions are special cards with hidden information on their backs. When you gain one, you can only look at the front. The front describes when the card flips, at which point you read and resolve the back immediately. Spells often require a Lore test to use and can have unpredictable back effects. Conditions represent ongoing states like being Blessed, Cursed, or in Debt. Some Conditions have reckoning effects that trigger during the Mythos Phase.

summary.title: Game Round Overview
bullets: Each round has 3 phases: Action, Encounter, Mythos
All investigators resolve each phase before moving to the next
Game continues until investigators win (solve 3 Mysteries) or lose

summary.title: Phase 1: The Action Phase
bullets: Each investigator performs exactly 2 actions per round
Start with Lead Investigator, proceed clockwise
6 possible actions: Travel, Rest, Trade, Prepare for Travel, Acquire Assets, Component Actions
Each action can only be performed once per round

bullets: Move to any adjacent space for free
Spend Train Tickets (brown paths) or Ship Tickets (blue paths) for extra moves

Gain 1 Train or Ship Ticket (must have matching path type)
Maximum of 2 travel tickets at any time

summary.title: Action: Acquire Assets
bullets: Must be on a City space with no Monster
Test Influence — successes = budget to spend on reserve Assets
Optional: take a Bank Loan (Debt Condition) for +2 successes
If no cards gained, may discard 1 card from the reserve

summary.title: Action: Component Actions
bullets: Use an 'Action:' ability on your Investigator sheet, Asset, Artifact, Spell, or Condition
One Component Action per component per round
Local Actions can be used by another investigator on same space

bullets: Each investigator resolves one encounter, starting with Lead Investigator
Monsters on your space = mandatory Combat Encounter first
No Monsters = choose a location encounter or token encounter

summary.title: Location Encounters

Resolve the effect matching your current space type
Named cities on the board hint at common encounter effects

Gate token = draw Other World Encounter card

Rumor token = resolve the matching Rumor Mythos card encounter

summary.title: Combat Encounters
bullets: Flip Monster token to read its stats
Will test first: horror minus successes = Sanity lost
Strength test second: damage minus successes = Health lost
Your Strength successes also deal Health to the Monster
Monster defeated when Health lost >= toughness, returned to cup

summary.title: How Tests Work
bullets: Roll dice equal to your skill value (minimum 1 die)
Each 5 or 6 = 1 success
Spend Clue tokens to reroll individual dice (1 Clue per reroll)
Only 1 card bonus may apply per test

summary.title: Complex Encounters
bullets: Expedition, Other World, and Special Encounters have 3 boxes
Resolve initial effect, then pass or fail effect based on test result
Other World Encounters can close Gates (discard Gate token)
Expedition token moves to the next card's space after resolving

summary.title: Phase 3: The Mythos Phase
bullets: Lead Investigator draws top Mythos card
Resolve icons in order: left to right, top to bottom
Only resolve icons actually shown on the card

summary.title: Advance Omen and Doom
bullets: Move Omen token 1 space clockwise
Advance Doom by 1 for each Gate matching the new Omen symbol
More Gates on board = faster Doom advancement

Spawn Gates: place Gate + 1 Monster on indicated space
Monster Surge: spawn extra Monsters at Omen-matching Gates

summary.title: Mythos Card Text Effects
bullets: Event cards: resolve immediately, then discard
Ongoing cards: stay in play near Ancient One sheet
Ongoing cards often include Rumor side quests with dire consequences

summary.title: Mysteries and Winning the Game
bullets: Solve 3 Mysteries to win
Each Mystery has unique conditions (spend Clues, close Gates, etc.)
New Mystery drawn immediately when one is solved

summary.title: The Ancient One Awakens
bullets: Doom reaches 0 = Ancient One awakens, flip sheet
Resolve Awakens effects (Azathoth: instant loss)
Other Ancient Ones: must solve Final Mystery + 3 regular Mysteries
Defeated investigators after awakening are permanently eliminated

summary.title: Losing the Game
bullets: Ancient One awakening effect causes immediate loss (e.g., Azathoth)
All investigators eliminated after awakening
Card effect causes loss (usually Rumor cards)
Mythos deck runs out when you need to draw

summary.title: Health, Sanity, and Defeated Investigators

Token moved to nearest City, laid on side with cause marker
Possessions stored on sheet for other investigators to retrieve

summary.title: Improvement Tokens and Skills
bullets: Improve a skill = gain matching Improvement token (+1 side up)
Second improvement of same skill = flip to +2 side

bullets: Hidden info on card backs — only read front when gained
Card describes when it flips; resolve back immediately
Spells: powerful but unpredictable (Lore test to use)
Conditions: ongoing states (Blessed, Cursed, Debt, etc.) with reckoning effects

walkthrough.title: Welcome and Meet the Investigators
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with three investigators: Silas Marsh the Sailor, Lily Chen the Martial Artist, and Jim Culver the Musician. Each investigator has different strengths, so you will see how the game plays out from multiple perspectives. Even if your group has different investigators, the concepts apply the same way. We are playing against Azathoth for this tutorial. Ready? Let's save the world.

walkthrough.title: The Board State
walkthrough.text: After setup, there is one Gate and one Monster on Arkham, and another Gate and Monster on space 14 near Istanbul. The Doom track starts at 15. Our first Mystery card requires us to close Gates matching the current Omen and spend Clues to place Eldritch tokens. A Clue token sits on Buenos Aires, and another on Tokyo. The investigators are spread across the board: Silas starts in Sydney, Lily in San Francisco, and Jim in Istanbul.

walkthrough.title: Silas's First Action: Prepare for Travel
walkthrough.text: Silas is in Sydney, a City space connected to both ship and train paths. He wants to eventually reach Tokyo to grab that Clue token. For his first action, Silas performs the Prepare for Travel action and takes a Ship Ticket. He now has the ticket he needs to move along ship paths toward his goal.

walkthrough.title: Silas's Second Action: Travel
walkthrough.text: For his second action, Silas performs a Travel action. He moves one space from Sydney to space 18, then spends his Ship Ticket to move one more space along the ship path to Tokyo. Now Silas is on Tokyo, right where the Clue token is sitting. During the Encounter Phase, he will be able to investigate that Clue. Smart planning got him exactly where he needed to be.

walkthrough.title: Why That Was a Good Move
walkthrough.text: Silas prioritized getting to a Clue token early. Clues are the most important resource in Eldritch Horror. They let you reroll dice during critical tests, and most Mysteries require spending Clues to solve them. Getting a Clue on turn one puts your team ahead. Silas also used his special ability: his Investigator sheet lets him move one space along a ship path, then perform one additional action. He is the fastest sailor in the game.

walkthrough.title: Lily's First Action: Travel
walkthrough.text: Lily Chen starts in San Francisco. There is a Monster and Gate on Arkham, just a couple of spaces away. Lily is the Martial Artist with 4 Strength and a .38 Revolver that gives +2 Strength in combat. She is the best fighter in the group. For her first action, she Travels from San Francisco to space 5.

walkthrough.title: Lily's Second Action: Travel Again? The Mistake
walkthrough.text: Lily wants to get to Arkham to fight that Monster. But wait, she already used her Travel action this turn. She tries to Travel again. The rules say each investigator can only perform each action once per round. Lily cannot Travel a second time. This is one of the most common mistakes new players make. Instead, she should have used Prepare for Travel to grab a Train Ticket and spent it during her first Travel action to reach Arkham in one go.

walkthrough.title: Lily's Actual Second Action: Rest
walkthrough.text: Since Lily cannot Travel again, she decides to Rest instead, recovering 1 Health and 1 Sanity. She is already at maximum for both, so nothing happens. She essentially wasted her second action. The lesson here is critical: plan both of your actions before you start. If you need to cover a lot of ground, Prepare for Travel first to get tickets, then Travel. Or use a Component Action to supplement your movement.

walkthrough.title: Jim's First Action: Acquire Assets
walkthrough.text: Jim Culver is in Istanbul, a City space. He decides to Acquire Assets. Jim has an Influence of 2, so he rolls 2 dice. He gets a 3 and a 6, that is one success. Looking at the reserve, there is a .45 Automatic with a value of 1, so Jim can afford it. He takes the weapon. Now Jim has some combat capability, which he will need because there is a Monster lurking on space 14 near him.

walkthrough.title: Jim's Second Action: Prepare for Travel
walkthrough.text: Jim wants to head toward that Gate on space 14, but he also needs to position for future turns. He takes the Prepare for Travel action and gains a Ship Ticket. Jim now has options for next round. Planning ahead for travel is essential in a game where the board is this large.

walkthrough.title: Encounter Phase: Silas in Tokyo
walkthrough.text: Now we enter the Encounter Phase. There are no Monsters on Silas's space, so he has a choice. He could do a location encounter in Tokyo, or he could encounter the Clue token. Clue tokens let you draw Research Encounter cards specific to your Ancient One. These encounters are complex and potentially very rewarding. Silas chooses the Clue token encounter. He draws a Research Encounter card for Azathoth and resolves the initial effect, which requires an Observation test.

walkthrough.title: Silas's Test: The Second Mistake
walkthrough.text: Silas has Observation 3, but the test has a minus-1 modifier, so he only rolls 2 dice. He rolls a 2 and a 4. Zero successes. He fails the test. Silas has a Clue token available. He could spend it to reroll one die, giving him another chance to roll a 5 or 6. But Silas decides to save his Clue for later because he thinks he might need it for the Mystery. He resolves the fail effect and loses 2 Sanity. That hurts.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: When to Spend Clues on Rerolls
walkthrough.text: Silas made a questionable call. Spending a Clue to reroll is almost always worth it during a Research Encounter, because passing that encounter often earns you a Clue or other reward that replaces what you spent. Hoarding Clues without using them means failing tests you could have passed. The general rule of thumb: spend Clues aggressively on important tests, especially Research Encounters and Mystery-related tests. You will find more Clues as the game goes on.

walkthrough.title: Encounter Phase: Lily on Wilderness Space 5
walkthrough.text: Lily is on space 5, a wilderness space with no Monster and no tokens. She performs a location encounter by drawing a General Encounter card and resolving the wilderness effect. The card gives her a Lore test. Lily's Lore is only 2. She rolls and fails. Not the best outcome, but location encounters on wilderness spaces are usually not game-changing. Next round, Lily needs to get to Arkham where she can fight that Monster and deal with the Gate.

walkthrough.title: Encounter Phase: Jim in Istanbul
walkthrough.text: Jim is in Istanbul with no Monsters on his space. He does a location encounter, drawing from the Europe encounter deck. Istanbul encounters often improve Influence, and Jim gets lucky. He resolves an Influence test, passes, and improves his Influence. He takes an Influence Improvement token and places it plus-1 side up next to his sheet. Jim's Influence is now 3 instead of 2, making future Acquire Assets actions more powerful.

walkthrough.title: The Mythos Phase
walkthrough.text: Now the Lead Investigator draws the top Mythos card. The card shows the Advance Omen icon and the Spawn Clues icon, plus a text effect. First, we Advance Omen. The Omen token moves one space clockwise to the blue symbol. We check: are there any Gates on the board matching blue? The Gate on Arkham shows a blue symbol. Doom advances by 1, moving from 15 to 14. Then we resolve Spawn Clues, placing a new Clue token on its indicated space. Finally, we resolve the card's Event text, which affects all investigators.

walkthrough.title: Jim's Mistake: Ignoring the Monster
walkthrough.text: Looking ahead to Round 2, Jim notices the Monster on space 14 between him and the Gate he needs to close. Jim decides to go around it instead of through it, adding two extra rounds of travel to avoid combat. Meanwhile, that Monster could spawn more through Monster Surge, and the Gate is advancing Doom every time the Omen matches. Avoiding Monsters entirely is a common beginner mistake. Jim has a .45 Automatic now. He should head straight for the Monster, fight it, then close the Gate.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Do Not Avoid Every Monster
walkthrough.text: In Eldritch Horror, Monsters block you from Resting and from performing location encounters. More importantly, they protect Gates. If you never fight, Gates stay open and Doom races toward zero. Your team needs at least one or two investigators geared for combat. Lily is your primary fighter, but Jim with his .45 Automatic can handle weaker Monsters too. Coordinate: send fighters to Monster-heavy spaces and scholars to Clue-heavy spaces.

walkthrough.title: Round 2 Preview
walkthrough.text: That concludes Round 1. Here is where you stand: Doom is at 14. You have solved zero of three Mysteries. Silas lost some Sanity but is near useful spaces. Lily needs to reach Arkham to fight the Monster and deal with that Gate. Jim should gear up and head toward space 14. Next round, plan your two actions carefully. Remember: each action only once per round. Prepare travel tickets, then Travel. Get to Clue tokens and Gates. Every round that passes without progress brings the Ancient One closer to awakening.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have now seen all three phases of a round, watched investigators Travel, Rest, Acquire Assets, resolve encounters, fight through the Mythos Phase, and learn from three common mistakes: trying to take the same action twice, hoarding Clues instead of spending them on rerolls, and avoiding Monsters when you should be engaging. You have got the fundamentals down. Time to play for real.

walkthrough.title: Before You Begin
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every term, condition, and investigator action in the game.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun, and may the stars be merciful.

summary.title: Welcome and Meet the Investigators
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 3 investigators: Silas Marsh, Lily Chen, Jim Culver
Playing against Azathoth (recommended first Ancient One)
Covers a complete first round from Action Phase through Mythos Phase

summary.title: The Board State
bullets: Gates + Monsters on Arkham and space 14
Doom starts at 15, first Mystery card drawn
Clue tokens on Buenos Aires and Tokyo

summary.title: Silas's First Action: Prepare for Travel
bullets: Silas is in Sydney, a City space
Takes Prepare for Travel action to gain a Ship Ticket
Planning ahead to reach the Clue on Tokyo

summary.title: Silas's Second Action: Travel
bullets: Moves from Sydney to space 18 (free move)
Spends Ship Ticket to move again to Tokyo
Now positioned on the Clue token for the Encounter Phase

summary.title: Why That Was a Good Move
bullets: Clues are the most important resource in the game
Used for rerolls, Mystery solving, and Rumor solving
Getting a Clue on turn 1 puts the team ahead

summary.title: Lily's First Action: Travel
bullets: Lily starts in San Francisco, heads toward Arkham's Monster
Lily has 4 Strength + .38 Revolver (+2) — best fighter in the group
Moves from San Francisco to space 5

summary.title: Lily's Second Action: Travel Again? The Mistake
bullets: Lily tries to Travel a second time — not allowed!
Each action can only be performed once per round
Should have used Prepare for Travel + Travel to go further

summary.title: Lily's Actual Second Action: Rest
bullets: Forced to pick a different action — Rests at full health (wasted)
Lesson: plan both actions before starting your turn
Use Prepare for Travel to get tickets, then Travel

summary.title: Jim's First Action: Acquire Assets
bullets: Jim tests Influence (2 dice), rolls 1 success
Takes a .45 Automatic (value 1) from the reserve
Now has combat capability for upcoming Monster fights

summary.title: Jim's Second Action: Prepare for Travel
bullets: Gains a Ship Ticket for future travel
Planning ahead is essential on a large board
Jim is positioning to reach the Gate on space 14

summary.title: Encounter Phase: Silas in Tokyo
bullets: No Monster = chooses between location encounter or Clue token
Picks the Clue token to draw a Research Encounter card
Research Encounters are complex: initial test, then pass/fail effect

summary.title: Silas's Test: The Second Mistake
bullets: Fails the Observation test (rolled 2 and 4)
Has a Clue he could spend to reroll but decides to save it
Loses 2 Sanity from the fail effect — a costly choice

summary.title: Lesson: When to Spend Clues on Rerolls
bullets: Spend Clues aggressively on important tests
Research Encounters often reward Clues — spending one is usually worth it
Hoarding Clues leads to failing tests you could have passed

summary.title: Encounter Phase: Lily on Wilderness Space 5
bullets: No Monster, no tokens — draws a General Encounter card
Resolves the wilderness effect, fails a Lore test
Next round: needs to reach Arkham to fight the Monster

summary.title: Encounter Phase: Jim in Istanbul
bullets: Draws from Europe encounter deck, resolves Istanbul encounter
Passes Influence test and improves his Influence skill (+1)
Jim's Influence is now 3 — better Acquire Assets rolls going forward

summary.title: The Mythos Phase
bullets: Advance Omen: move token clockwise, check Gates for Doom advance
Gate matches new Omen symbol = Doom advances by 1 (15 to 14)
Spawn Clues and resolve the card's Event text

summary.title: Jim's Mistake: Ignoring the Monster
bullets: Jim plans to go around the Monster instead of through it
Adds 2 extra rounds of travel while Gate stays open and Doom advances
He has a weapon — he should fight the Monster and close the Gate

summary.title: Lesson: Do Not Avoid Every Monster
bullets: Monsters block Rest and location encounters
Monsters protect Gates that advance Doom
Send fighters to Monsters, scholars to Clues — coordinate your team

summary.title: Round 2 Preview
bullets: Doom at 14, zero Mysteries solved, round 2 begins
Priority: Lily to Arkham, Jim to space 14, Silas to recover
Plan both actions carefully — each action only once per round

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: Practiced all 3 phases: Action, Encounter, Mythos
Saw 3 common mistakes: duplicate actions, hoarding Clues, avoiding Monsters
You have the fundamentals — time to play for real!

summary.title: Before You Begin
bullets: Check out General Tips for strategy basics
Reference the Appendix for terms and conditions
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime — tap Q&A for instant answers

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest beginner traps. First, ignoring Gates. Every Gate on the board is a ticking time bomb that advances Doom whenever the Omen matches. Leaving Gates open is the fastest way to lose. Second, hoarding Clues instead of spending them on rerolls during critical tests. Third, spreading your investigators too thin across the map instead of coordinating. Fourth, neglecting Rumor Mythos cards. Rumors have deadlines, and ignoring them often leads to an instant loss or catastrophic penalty. Keep these in mind from your first game.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Closing Gates
walkthrough.text: Gates are the engine of your doom, literally. Every Mythos Phase, the Omen advances and Doom ticks down by one for each Gate that matches the new Omen symbol. With two or three Gates open, Doom can drop by two or three per round. You need investigators actively moving toward Gates, fighting the Monsters guarding them, and encountering the Gate tokens to draw Other World Encounter cards. Closing a Gate removes the token from the board and stops it from advancing Doom. Make Gate closure your top priority in every game.

walkthrough.title: Clue Management
walkthrough.text: Clues are your most versatile resource. They let you reroll dice during tests, they are required to solve most Mysteries, and they are needed to solve Rumors. Do not hoard them, but do not waste them either. Spend Clues on rerolls when the stakes are high: combat against a dangerous Monster, a Research Encounter that could earn you rewards, or a test tied to the current Mystery. Do not spend Clues on trivial location encounters where failure is not dangerous. Keep at least one Clue on hand for emergencies, but cycle through them aggressively. You will always find more.

walkthrough.title: Specialize Your Investigators
walkthrough.text: Each investigator has different skill values and abilities. Do not try to make everyone a generalist. Send your high-Strength investigators like Lily Chen toward Monsters and Gates. Send your high-Lore investigators toward Spell-related tasks and Other World Encounters. Send your high-Influence investigators to cities to Acquire Assets for the team. Jim Culver's ability heals Sanity for everyone on his space, so keep him near investigators who are taking Sanity damage. Match jobs to skills.

walkthrough.title: Plan Two Actions Ahead
walkthrough.text: Remember that each investigator performs exactly two actions per round, and you cannot repeat the same action. This means every turn requires planning. If you need to reach a distant space, use Prepare for Travel on one action and Travel on the other. If you want to buy Assets, make sure you are on a City space with no Monster before the Action Phase begins. Think about where you need to be during the Encounter Phase and work backward. The players who plan ahead consistently outperform those who react in the moment.

walkthrough.title: Read the Mystery Card Carefully
walkthrough.text: Every Mystery card spells out exactly what you need to do to solve it. Some require spending Clues at specific spaces. Some require closing Gates that match the current Omen. Some require defeating certain Monsters. Read the Mystery at the start of each round and coordinate your team's actions around completing it. Do not just wander the board doing random encounters. Every action should be moving you closer to solving the active Mystery.

walkthrough.title: Do Not Ignore Rumor Mythos Cards
walkthrough.text: When an Ongoing Mythos card with a Rumor effect enters play, it places a Rumor token on a specific space. These Rumors have reckoning effects that trigger every Mythos Phase and often have catastrophic fail conditions, including causing the investigators to immediately lose the game. Treat Rumors as urgent side quests. Send someone to the Rumor space to resolve it before the deadline. Letting a Rumor fester while you focus solely on Mysteries is a recipe for disaster.

walkthrough.title: Manage Health and Sanity
walkthrough.text: When an investigator reaches zero Health or zero Sanity, they are defeated. Doom advances by one, and the player has to start over with a new investigator who has lost all their accumulated items and improvements. That is devastating. Rest when you need to, even if it feels like wasting a turn. Carry healing items when possible. Jim Culver's ability to heal Sanity for investigators on his space is extremely valuable. Keep track of your teammates' Health and Sanity and heal proactively rather than reactively.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Leaving Gates open — they advance Doom every Omen match
Hoarding Clues instead of spending on important rerolls
Spreading investigators too thin instead of coordinating
Ignoring Rumor Mythos cards — they can cause instant loss

summary.title: Prioritize Closing Gates
bullets: Gates are the primary Doom engine — close them as fast as possible
Move to Gates, fight the Monster guard, encounter the Gate token
Other World Encounters can close Gates (discard Gate token)
Fewer Gates = slower Doom = more time to solve Mysteries

summary.title: Clue Management
bullets: Spend Clues on rerolls during high-stakes tests
Save Clues for Mystery requirements and Rumor solving
Do not waste on trivial encounters but do not hoard either
You will always find more Clues through encounters and spawns

summary.title: Specialize Your Investigators
bullets: High Strength = send to Monsters and Gates (Lily Chen, Mark Harrigan)
High Lore = send to Other World Encounters and Spells (Jacqueline Fine, Norman Withers)
High Influence = send to cities for Asset acquisition (Charlie Kane)
Match investigator abilities to team needs

summary.title: Plan Two Actions Ahead
bullets: Cannot repeat the same action in one round
Combine Prepare for Travel + Travel to cover distance
Think about Encounter Phase positioning during Action Phase
Planning beats reacting in every round

summary.title: Read the Mystery Card Carefully
bullets: Each Mystery has specific conditions to solve
Coordinate all investigators around the active Mystery
Every action should move you closer to solving it
Do not wander — focus is how you win

summary.title: Do Not Ignore Rumor Mythos Cards
bullets: Rumors have reckoning effects that trigger every Mythos Phase
Some Rumors cause instant loss if not resolved in time
Treat Rumors as urgent side quests
Send someone to the Rumor space immediately

summary.title: Manage Health and Sanity
bullets: Zero Health or Sanity = defeated, Doom advances by 1
Losing an investigator means losing all items and improvements
Rest proactively — do not wait until you are nearly dead
Jim Culver's Sanity healing ability is extremely valuable

walkthrough.title: Gate Closure Priority System
walkthrough.text: Not all Gates are equally dangerous. A Gate whose symbol appears twice on the Omen track will match the Omen more frequently, advancing Doom more often. Prioritize closing Gates that match the most common Omen symbols. Also consider Gate location: a Gate in a remote wilderness space is harder to reach and may not be worth the travel time if another Gate is in a city you can reach in one round. Calculate the opportunity cost of travel time versus Doom advancement risk when deciding which Gate to close first.

walkthrough.title: Investigator Synergies
walkthrough.text: Some investigators work exceptionally well together. Lily Chen paired with Jim Culver is a powerhouse: Lily fights Monsters while Jim heals her Sanity loss from horror damage. Silas Marsh's ship movement ability pairs with Norman Withers's Observation and Lore, letting them rapidly reach and close Gates in sea-adjacent areas. Jacqueline Fine's ability to reroll during Mythos Phase effects protects the whole team from catastrophic Mythos cards. When choosing investigators, think about how their abilities complement each other rather than picking the strongest individuals.

walkthrough.title: The Doom Clock and Pacing
walkthrough.text: With Azathoth at Doom 15, you have roughly 8 to 12 rounds depending on Gate management. The Mythos deck has three stages of increasing difficulty. Stage I is relatively mild, Stage II introduces harder challenges, and Stage III is brutal. Your first three to four rounds should be spent acquiring gear, collecting Clues, and solving your first Mystery. Rounds five through eight should focus on closing Gates and solving the second Mystery. The final rounds are a sprint to solve Mystery three before Doom hits zero or the deck runs out.

walkthrough.title: Spell Strategy
walkthrough.text: Spells are high-risk, high-reward. Each Spell card has a hidden back effect that triggers when you flip it, and it can range from amazing to devastating. Incantation Spells, gained at Arkham, are generally safer and focus on investigation. Ritual Spells, gained at Buenos Aires, tend to be more powerful but riskier. The key to Spell strategy is having a high-Lore investigator who can reliably pass the Lore test to use them. Norman Withers with Lore 4 or Jacqueline Fine with Lore 3 and her reroll ability are ideal Spell users. Never rely on Spells as your only plan because the back effect might ruin your turn.

walkthrough.title: Asset Economy
walkthrough.text: The reserve only holds four Assets at a time, and powerful weapons, tomes, and allies cycle through unpredictably. When you see a high-value item in the reserve that your team needs, send your best Influence investigator to grab it immediately. Do not wait. Charlie Kane with his high Influence or any investigator who has improved Influence at Istanbul can reliably acquire expensive Assets. Bank Loans give you plus-2 successes but come with a Debt Condition that has a nasty reckoning effect. Use Bank Loans only when the Asset is critical, like a weapon for your fighter or a powerful Tome for your scholar.

walkthrough.title: Positioning and Map Control
walkthrough.text: The Eldritch Horror map has natural chokepoints and key cities. Arkham grants Incantation Spells. Buenos Aires grants Ritual Spells. The five improvement cities (San Francisco, Istanbul, Rome, Shanghai, Sydney) should be visited early to boost your investigators' skills. Position your investigators in a triangle pattern that covers the most likely Gate spawn locations. Having one investigator in the Americas, one in Europe/Africa, and one in Asia/Australia means you can respond to threats anywhere on the board within two rounds.

walkthrough.title: Ancient One-Specific Strategies
walkthrough.text: Each Ancient One demands a different approach. Azathoth is straightforward: manage Doom above all else because awakening means instant loss. There is no Final Mystery to fall back on. Cthulhu floods the board with Monsters through his Cultist mechanic, so you need strong combat capability. Shub-Niggurath spawns Epic Monsters that guard her Final Mystery. Yog-Sothoth manipulates the Mythos deck and Gates unpredictably. Study your Ancient One's sheet before the game starts and build your team composition and strategy around its specific threats.

walkthrough.title: Clue Efficiency and Mystery Solving
walkthrough.text: Most Mysteries require either spending Clues, placing Eldritch tokens, or both. Calculate the minimum number of Clues your team needs to solve the active Mystery, then add two or three for rerolls on the tests involved. Distribute Clue-gathering duties across your team. Research Encounters from Clue tokens are the best source, but certain city encounters and card effects also generate Clues. When a Mystery requires closing Gates matching the current Omen, track which Omen symbol is coming next and position your investigator at the matching Gate before the Mythos Phase.

walkthrough.title: Dealing with Epic Monsters
walkthrough.text: Epic Monsters are the toughest enemies in the game. They cannot be moved, discarded, or returned to the Monster cup. They can only be defeated by dealing Health damage equal to their toughness through combat. Some Ancient Ones spawn specific Epic Monsters during the game or when they awaken. When facing an Epic Monster, send your best-equipped fighter with weapons that grant plus Strength bonuses. Use Spells and Assets that deal direct damage. Coordinate multiple investigators to whittle down its Health over multiple rounds if necessary. Epic Monsters are often tied to the Final Mystery, so you cannot avoid them.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Triage
walkthrough.text: When Doom is below 5 and you have not solved your third Mystery, it is time for triage. Stop all non-essential activities. Every action must directly contribute to solving the Mystery or closing Gates to buy time. Abandon Clue tokens in far-off locations. Stop improving skills. Stop acquiring Assets unless the specific Asset in the reserve directly solves your problem. If a teammate is about to be defeated, consider whether saving them or letting them fall and sending the new investigator to a more useful location is the better strategic play. The endgame of Eldritch Horror is ruthless and requires cold, calculated decisions.

summary.title: Gate Closure Priority System
bullets: Prioritize Gates whose symbols appear most frequently on the Omen track
Factor in travel time vs Doom risk when choosing which Gate to close
City Gates are easier to reach; remote Gates may not be worth the trip
Closing even 1 Gate can reduce Doom advancement by 1 per matching Omen

summary.title: Investigator Synergies
bullets: Lily Chen + Jim Culver: fighter + Sanity healer combo
Silas Marsh + Norman Withers: fast sea travel + strong Lore for Gates
Jacqueline Fine: reroll ability protects team from bad Mythos draws
Build teams with complementary abilities, not just strong individuals

summary.title: The Doom Clock and Pacing
bullets: Azathoth at Doom 15 = roughly 8-12 rounds depending on Gates
Rounds 1-4: gear up, collect Clues, solve Mystery 1
Rounds 5-8: close Gates, solve Mystery 2
Final rounds: sprint to Mystery 3 before Doom hits 0

summary.title: Spell Strategy
bullets: Spells are high-risk, high-reward with hidden back effects
Incantation Spells (Arkham) = safer; Ritual Spells (Buenos Aires) = stronger
Best on high-Lore investigators: Norman Withers (4), Jacqueline Fine (3+reroll)
Never rely on Spells as your only plan

summary.title: Asset Economy
bullets: Grab critical Assets immediately — reserve cycles unpredictably
High-Influence investigators should shop for the team
Bank Loans (+2 successes) are useful but Debt Condition has reckoning risk
Weapons for fighters, Tomes for scholars — distribute strategically

summary.title: Positioning and Map Control
bullets: Key cities: Arkham (Incantation), Buenos Aires (Ritual), 5 improvement cities
Triangle positioning: Americas, Europe/Africa, Asia/Australia
Respond to any threat within 2 rounds with good positioning
Visit improvement cities early to boost skills permanently

summary.title: Ancient One-Specific Strategies
bullets: Azathoth: manage Doom above all — awakening = instant loss
Cthulhu: heavy combat needed, floods board with Cultists/Monsters
Shub-Niggurath: Epic Monster spawns, requires sustained combat
Yog-Sothoth: unpredictable Gate and Mythos manipulation

summary.title: Clue Efficiency and Mystery Solving
bullets: Calculate minimum Clues needed for Mystery + add 2-3 for rerolls
Research Encounters are the best Clue source
Track upcoming Omen symbols to position at matching Gates
Distribute Clue-gathering duties across the team

summary.title: Dealing with Epic Monsters
bullets: Cannot be moved, discarded, or returned to Monster cup
Defeated only by dealing Health damage >= toughness
Send best-equipped fighter with +Strength weapons and Spells
May require multiple investigators over multiple rounds

summary.title: Endgame Triage
bullets: Doom below 5 = stop all non-essential activities
Every action must directly solve the Mystery or close Gates
Abandon distant Clues, skip improvements, skip shopping
Make ruthless calculated decisions — endgame is unforgiving

entries.definition: The first phase of each game round. Each investigator performs exactly two actions, starting with the Lead Investigator and proceeding clockwise. Each action can only be performed once per round.

The second phase of each game round. Each investigator resolves one encounter. If Monsters are present on an investigator's space, Combat Encounters must be resolved first. Otherwise, the investigator chooses a location or token encounter.

The third and final phase of each game round. The Lead Investigator draws and resolves the top card of the Mythos deck. Effects include Advance Omen, Reckoning, Spawn Gates, Monster Surge, Spawn Clues, and text effects.

Action: Move your Investigator token to one adjacent space. After moving, you may spend travel tickets to move additional spaces along matching path types (Train Tickets for brown train paths, Ship Tickets for blue ship paths).
Action: Recover 1 Health and 1 Sanity, up to your maximums. Cannot be performed if there is a Monster on your space.
Action: Exchange possessions (Assets, Artifacts, Clues, Spells, travel tickets) with another investigator on the same space. Both players must agree to the trade.

Action: If on a City space, gain one travel ticket of your choice. Can only gain a Train Ticket if a train path connects to your space, or a Ship Ticket if a ship path connects. Maximum 2 travel tickets at any time.

Action: If on a City space with no Monster, test Influence. Gain Asset cards from the reserve with total value equal to or less than the number of successes. If no cards are gained, you may discard one reserve card. Refill the reserve afterward.

Action: Perform an action printed on one of your components (Investigator sheet, Asset, Artifact, Spell, or Condition). Preceded by the word 'Action' in bold. One Component Action per component per round. 'Local Action' can be used by another investigator on the same space.

Roll dice equal to your skill value plus or minus modifiers (minimum 1 die). Each die showing 5 or 6 is a success. If at least one success is rolled, the test is passed. The total number of successes is the test result.

Immediately after rolling dice during a test, you may spend one Clue token to reroll one die. You may repeat this multiple times, spending one Clue per reroll. Determine pass/fail after all rerolls are complete.
Card effects that provide a bonus to a skill during a test. Only one skill bonus from card effects may be used per test. If multiple bonuses apply, use the highest.

The track on the game board measuring time until the Ancient One awakens. When Doom advances, move the token toward 0. When Doom retreats, move it away from 0. When Doom reaches 0, the Ancient One awakens.
A circular track whose primary purpose is to advance Doom. When the Omen token advances one space clockwise, Doom advances by 1 for each Gate on the board matching the new Omen symbol.
A Mythos Phase effect. Move the Omen token one space clockwise. Then advance Doom by 1 for each Gate on the game board whose symbol matches the Omen token's new position.
A Mythos Phase effect. Resolve all effects marked with the red reckoning icon in this order: Monsters, Ancient One sheet, Ongoing Mythos cards, then investigator possessions and Conditions.
A Mythos Phase effect. Spawn a number of Gates as indicated on the Reference card. Draw Gate tokens from the stack and place them on indicated spaces. Each Gate spawns one Monster on the same space.
A Mythos Phase effect. At each Gate matching the current Omen symbol, spawn the number of Monsters listed on the Reference card. If no Gates match, spawn one Gate instead.
A Mythos Phase effect. Spawn a number of Clues as indicated on the Reference card. Draw Clue tokens from the pool and place them facedown on their indicated spaces.
A Mythos card with the Event trait. Resolve its text effect immediately, then discard the card. Events are one-time effects that do not stay in play.
A Mythos card with the Ongoing trait. Place it near the Ancient One sheet. It remains in play, affecting the game until it is discarded by a game effect or solved. Often includes Rumor effects.

A token representing a tear in reality. Gates are placed on the game board and advance Doom when the Omen matches their symbol. Investigators close Gates by resolving Other World Encounter cards on Gate spaces.

Tokens representing secrets and lore about the Ancient One. Placed facedown on board spaces. Investigators on a Clue's space can encounter it to draw a Research Encounter card. Clues are also spent to reroll dice and solve Mysteries.

Tokens drawn from the Monster cup and placed on the board. The front shows the Monster name and type. The back shows combat stats: Will test modifier, horror, Strength test modifier, damage, toughness, and any special effects.
Generic tokens placed on cards (usually Mysteries) to track progress. Many Mysteries require accumulating Eldritch tokens to solve them.

Placed on the board by Ongoing Mythos cards with Rumor effects. An investigator on a Rumor token's space can encounter it to attempt to solve the Rumor. Unsolved Rumors often have catastrophic reckoning effects.

Tokens placed below an Investigator sheet to permanently boost a skill. First improvement shows +1 side up. Second improvement of the same skill flips to +2 side. Six types: Lore, Influence, Observation, Strength, Will.
Tokens that allow extra movement during the Travel action. Train Tickets move along brown train paths, Ship Tickets along blue ship paths. Maximum of 2 tickets held at any time.
Given to one player at the start of the game. The Lead Investigator resolves phases first and breaks disputes. Passes to another player if the Lead Investigator is defeated.
Placed on the game board to mark locations relevant to the active Mystery card's requirements.

Represents an investigator's physical well-being. When an effect causes Health loss, discard Health tokens. When Health reaches zero, the investigator is defeated. Maximum Health is listed on the Investigator sheet.

Represents an investigator's mental stability. When an effect causes Sanity loss, discard Sanity tokens. When Sanity reaches zero, the investigator is defeated. Maximum Sanity is listed on the Investigator sheet.
One of five investigator skills. Used for Spell-related tests, some Other World Encounters, and various encounter cards. Tested with the bracket symbol. Key improvement city: Shanghai.
One of five investigator skills. Used for the Acquire Assets action and social encounters. Tested with the bracket symbol. Key improvement city: Istanbul.
One of five investigator skills. Used for investigation-related tests, searching for clues, and perception challenges. Tested with the bracket symbol. Key improvement city: San Francisco.
One of five investigator skills. Used for the Strength test in combat to deal damage and resist physical harm. Tested with the bracket symbol. Key improvement city: Sydney.
One of five investigator skills. Used for the Will test in combat to resist horror (Sanity loss) and for mental fortitude challenges. Tested with the bracket symbol. Key improvement city: Rome.

Mandatory encounter against each Monster on your space during the Encounter Phase. Flip the Monster token facedown. Resolve a Will test (horror) then a Strength test (damage and dealing damage). Monster defeated when Health lost equals or exceeds its toughness.
Draw a card from the encounter deck matching your space's region (America, Europe, Asia/Australia, or General). Resolve the effect matching your space type. Available when no Monsters are present.
A complex encounter drawn when investigating a Clue token. Uses the Ancient One's Research Encounter deck. Has initial, pass, and fail effects. Often rewards Clues, Spells, or other valuable items.
A complex encounter drawn when encountering a Gate token. Has initial, pass, and fail effects. Can instruct the investigator to 'close this Gate,' discarding the Gate token from the space.

A complex encounter drawn when encountering the Active Expedition token. Rewarding but dangerous. After resolving, the Active Expedition token moves to the space matching the new top card of the Expedition deck.
Expedition, Other World, and Special Encounters. Each card has three boxes: initial effect (top), pass effect, and fail effect. Resolve initial test first, then the matching result box.
A Monster stat on the back of its token. During the Will test in combat, if the Monster's horror exceeds the number of successes rolled, the investigator loses Sanity equal to the difference.
A Monster stat on the back of its token. During the Strength test in combat, if the Monster's damage exceeds the number of successes rolled, the investigator loses Health equal to the difference.
A Monster stat indicating how much Health damage it must take to be defeated. When a Monster has lost Health equal to or greater than its toughness, it is defeated and returned to the Monster cup.

Extremely powerful Monsters marked by red color and a clipped lower-left corner. Cannot be moved, discarded, or returned to the Monster cup. Defeated only by dealing Health damage equal to toughness, then returned to the game box. Spawned by specific named effects, never placed in the Monster cup.

Some effects say 'A Monster ambushes you.' Draw one random Monster from the cup and immediately encounter it in combat. After combat, return the Monster to the cup regardless of whether it was defeated.

An opaque container holding all normal (non-epic) Monster tokens. When a Monster is spawned, draw randomly from the cup. When a Monster is defeated or discarded, return it to the cup and shake to re-randomize.

Cards representing items, allies, and services. Gained through the Acquire Assets action or encounter effects. Each has a value (cost), name, traits, and effect. Placed faceup near your Investigator sheet.
Rare and powerful cards gained through encounters. Similar to Assets but typically stronger. Cannot be purchased through Acquire Assets. Placed faceup near your Investigator sheet.

Cards with hidden information on the back. Placed faceup when gained (only read the front). Often have an Action requiring a Lore test. The front describes when to flip; the back effect can be beneficial or harmful. Incantation Spells are gained at Arkham, Ritual Spells at Buenos Aires.

Cards representing ongoing states affecting an investigator. Hidden back information like Spells. The front describes when to flip (often during reckoning). Examples include Debt, Blessed, Cursed, Poisoned, and others.

Cards specific to each Ancient One describing how investigators can win. Three Mysteries must be solved to win. Each describes unique conditions such as spending Clues, closing Gates, or defeating Monsters. A new Mystery is drawn when one is solved.

Cards drawn during the Mythos Phase. Come in three colors (green, yellow, blue) and three stages. Contain icons for effects and a text effect (Event or Ongoing). The Mythos deck is constructed during setup per the Ancient One's instructions.
A card listing the number of Gates to spawn, Clues to spawn, and Monsters for Monster Surge based on the current number of players. Keep this card visible during the Mythos Phase.

An investigator who reaches zero Health or zero Sanity. Doom advances by 1. Token moved to nearest City, laid on side. Conditions, Health, Sanity, and Improvements discarded. Possessions stored for retrieval. Player picks new investigator at end of Mythos Phase.

A more severe form of defeat caused by specific game effects. The investigator only performs steps 1 and 4 of the defeated process (advance Doom and pass Lead Investigator). All possessions, tokens, and the Investigator sheet are removed from the game entirely. Player chooses a new investigator at end of Mythos Phase.

An investigator who has been Delayed tips their Investigator token on its side. During the next Action Phase, instead of performing actions, they stand up their token and are no longer Delayed. Delayed investigators lose an entire round of actions.

After the Ancient One awakens, defeated or devoured investigators are permanently eliminated instead of being replaced. The player can no longer participate. If all players are eliminated, investigators lose the game.

A Condition card. Provides a beneficial ongoing effect while held. The front describes when to flip. The back resolves when triggered. Being Blessed is one of the most desirable Conditions in the game.
A harmful Condition. Imposes negative effects on the investigator. Some effects prevent you from gaining Blessed while Cursed. The back side resolves when the flip condition is met.

A Condition gained from taking a Bank Loan during Acquire Assets. Provides +2 successes but has a reckoning effect: 'Local Action: Test Influence. If you pass, discard this card. Reckoning: Some men have come to collect on your debt. Flip this card.'
When performing an Acquire Assets action, you may gain a Debt Condition to immediately add 2 successes to your test result. Printed on the game board near the reserve.
Four faceup Asset cards at the bottom-left of the game board. Investigators can acquire these through the Acquire Assets action. Refilled from the Asset deck whenever cards are taken or discarded.

Named spaces on the board representing major cities (Arkham, London, Tokyo, etc.). Required for Prepare for Travel and Acquire Assets actions. Each city has a text effect describing common encounter themes.
Unnamed numbered spaces on land between cities. General Encounter cards resolve the 'Wilderness' effect on these spaces.
Unnamed numbered spaces on water between land areas. General Encounter cards resolve the 'Sea' effect on these spaces. Only accessible via Ship paths.
Brown-colored paths connecting spaces on the game board. Investigators can spend Train Tickets to move along these paths during the Travel action.
Blue-colored paths connecting spaces on the game board, typically crossing bodies of water. Investigators can spend Ship Tickets to move along these paths during the Travel action.

The cosmic horror entity the investigators are trying to stop. Each Ancient One has unique Mysteries, Cultist Monsters, a Doom value, Mythos deck configuration, and Awakens effects. The four base game Ancient Ones are Azathoth, Cthulhu, Shub-Niggurath, and Yog-Sothoth.

The Daemon Sultan. Starting Doom: approximately 15. The most straightforward Ancient One. When Azathoth awakens, the world is devoured and investigators immediately lose. There is no Final Mystery. Recommended for first games.

The Great Old One sleeping in R'lyeh. Floods the board with Cultist and Deep One Monsters. Awakening introduces a Final Mystery and additional Monster-spawning mechanics. Requires strong combat capability.

The Black Goat of the Woods With a Thousand Young. Spawns the Shub-Niggurath Epic Monster when awakening. Cultists have reckoning effects that move them toward the Epic Monster. Final Mystery: defeat the Shub-Niggurath Epic Monster.
The Key and the Gate. Manipulates Gates and the fabric of reality. Mechanics involve Gate interactions and dimensional rifts. Final Mystery requires complex Gate-related tasks.
A Monster type specific to each Ancient One. Cultist stats and reckoning effects are listed on the Ancient One sheet. When a Cultist would be spawned but none remain, Doom advances by 1 instead.

A special Mystery on the back of the Ancient One sheet that investigators must solve after the Ancient One awakens. Required in addition to the three regular Mysteries. Usually involves confronting the Ancient One directly. Azathoth has no Final Mystery (instant loss on awakening).

Meeting the conditions described on a Mystery card. As soon as a Mystery is solved, draw a new one. When three Mysteries are solved (plus the Final Mystery if the Ancient One has awakened), investigators win the game.
Meeting the conditions described on a Rumor Mythos card, often by spending Clues at the Rumor token's location. When solved, discard the Mythos card and corresponding Rumor token.

The Sailor. Starts in Sydney. Special ability: Action to move 1 space along a Ship path, then perform 1 additional action. Strong Will and Strength, good for combat and sea travel. Starting possession: Fishing Net Asset.

The Martial Artist. Starts in San Francisco. Special ability: When she improves a skill, she may immediately improve that skill again. High Strength (4), excellent fighter. Starting possession: .38 Revolver. Spend Health or Sanity to recover the other.

The Musician. Starts in Istanbul. Special ability: Action to let each investigator on his space recover 1 Sanity. Also resolves 1 additional die during Encounter Phase tests. Starting possession: varies.
The Psychic. Has the ability to reroll dice during certain tests and Mythos Phase effects. High Lore, making her excellent for Spell use and Other World Encounters. A strong support investigator.

The Astronomer. High Lore (4), strong at Spell use and Other World Encounters. Abilities relate to observing cosmic phenomena and gaining knowledge. Pairs well with investigators who can get him to Gate spaces safely.
The Soldier. High Strength and combat-focused abilities. Excellent at fighting Monsters and surviving Combat Encounters. A frontline fighter who should be sent to the most dangerous spaces.

The Politician. High Influence, making him the best investigator for Acquire Assets actions. Can acquire expensive items for the team. Support role: shops for gear and distributes it via Trade actions.

The Actress. Versatile investigator with abilities that let her adapt to different situations. Can change roles as needed. Her encounter on the back of her Investigator sheet references her theatrical background.

To place a token (Gate, Monster, or Clue) on the game board. Gates are drawn from the Gate stack. Monsters are drawn from the Monster cup. Clues are drawn from the Clue pool. Each placed on the space indicated by the token or effect.

When an Other World Encounter card instructs an investigator to 'close this Gate,' the Gate token is discarded from the investigator's space. Closing Gates is the primary way to slow Doom advancement.
Move the Doom token away from 0, buying investigators more time. Some encounter effects and Mystery completions retreat Doom. This is the opposite of advancing Doom.
Move the Doom token toward 0. Occurs when the Omen matches Gates, when an investigator is defeated, and through various card effects. When Doom reaches 0, the Ancient One awakens.

An investigator's Assets, Artifacts, Spells, Clues, and travel tickets. Possessions can be traded and are stored on a defeated investigator's sheet for retrieval. Conditions, Health, Sanity, and Improvement tokens are not possessions.
When calculating half of any number in Eldritch Horror, always round up. For example, half of 5 is 3.
When an effect refers to a random space, draw one Clue token from the Clue pool and use the space indicated on the back. Discard the drawn Clue token afterward.

During setup, follow the Ancient One's instructions specifying the number and color of Mythos cards per stage. Shuffle each stage separately, then stack Stage III on bottom, Stage II in middle, Stage I on top. The deck gets harder as you dig deeper.
